title: Erdős–Hajnal Set Mapping Problem
statement_text: Let $p(m,k,l)$ be the maximum $p$ such that for every mapping $f:\binom{M}{k}\to\binom{M}{l}$ with $|M|=m$ and $X\cap f(X)=\emptyset$ for all $X\in\binom{M}{k}$, there is a subset $P\subset M$ with $|P|=p$ and $f(X)\cap P=\emptyset$ for all $X\in\binom{P}{k}$. Determine the exact dependence of $p(m,k,l)$ on $m$.
context_text: In 1958, Erdős and Hajnal proved $cm^{1/(k+1)}\leq p(m,k,l)\leq c'(m\log m)^{1/k}$ and asked for a more exact determination. Spencer (1972) improved the lower bound to $p(m,k,l)\geq cm^{1/k}$, but the upper bound had not been improved for decades.
paper_contribution: Solves the problem when $l$ is sufficiently large as a function of $k$, showing $p(m,k,(k-1)!)=\Theta(m^{1/k})$, where the implied constants depend only on $k$.
paper_title: Short proofs of some extremal results II

title: Caro's Set Mapping Question on q(m,k,d)
statement_text: Let $q(m,k,d)$ be the maximum $q$ such that for every mapping $f:\binom{M}{k}\to\binom{M}{k}$ with $|M|=m$ and $|X\cap f(X)|\leq d$ for all $X\in\binom{M}{k}$, there is a subset $Q\subset M$ with $|Q|=q$ and $f(X)\not\subset Q$ for any $X\in\binom{Q}{k}$. Estimate $q(m,k,d)$; in particular, determine whether the logarithmic factor in the upper bound $q(m,k,d)\leq c'(m\log m)^t$ is necessary.
context_text: Caro proved $cm^t\leq q(m,k,d)\leq c'(m\log m)^t$ where $t=(k-d)/(2k-d-1)$. The problem asks whether the logarithmic gap between the bounds can be closed.
paper_contribution: Partially answers the question by removing the log factor from the upper bound when $k=2$, proving $q(m,2,1)\leq c_1 m^{1/2}$ and $q(m,2,0)\leq c_2 m^{2/3}$.
notes: PDF source — the formula for $t$ is garbled in the extracted text; value $t=(k-d)/(2k-d-1)$ inferred from surrounding context.
paper_title: Short proofs of some extremal results II

title: Bollobás–Erdős Problem on Large C4-free Subgraphs
statement_text: Does every graph with $m$ edges contain a $C_4$-free subgraph with $\Omega(m^{3/4})$ edges? More precisely, determine $f(m, C_4)$, the size of the largest $C_4$-free subgraph guaranteed in any graph with $m$ edges.
context_text: The problem of estimating $f(m,\mathcal{H})$ was first raised by Bollobás and Erdős at a 1966 workshop. Erdős later conjectured the answer is $\Theta(m^{2/3})$ based on an example due to Folkman and private communication from Szemerédi.
paper_contribution: Determines $f(m, K_{r,r})=\Theta(m^{r/(r+1)})$ for all $r\geq 2$ via Theorems 3.1 and 3.3, which as the special case $r=2$ (noting $K_{2,2}\cong C_4$) confirms Erdős's conjecture $f(m,C_4)=\Theta(m^{2/3})$.
paper_title: Short proofs of some extremal results II

title: Open Problem: Reducing l to 1 in the Set Mapping Theorem
statement_text: Determine whether $l$ can be decreased to $1$ for all $k$; that is, whether $p(m,k,1)=\Theta(m^{1/k})$.
context_text: Theorem 2.1 establishes $p(m,k,(k-1)!)=\Theta(m^{1/k})$. The case $l=(k-1)!$ is noted to be best possible when $k=2$ (independently solved earlier by Füredi). The authors remark it would be very interesting to determine whether $l$ can be decreased to $1$ for all $k$.
notes: Stated in running prose: 'It would be very interesting to determine whether l can be decreased to 1 for all k.' No labelled environment.
paper_title: Short proofs of some extremal results II
